journal_entry.description: Short-term loan advanced to business partner
journal_entry.rationale: Target advances a $2,000,000 short-term loan (1-year term) to a business partner. The loan is recorded as a current asset (Loans Receivable - Short-term) and cash decreases. Interest at 4.5% per annum will be recognized as revenue monthly when earned; this entry records only the principal disbursement.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Loan disbursement increases Loans Receivable (asset) and decreases Chequing Account (asset). Interest revenue will be recognized separately each month as earned under the accrual basis. No tax applies to financial transactions (loan principal).

output_credit_classifier.reason: Loan disbursement increases Loans Receivable (asset) and decreases Chequing Account (asset). Interest revenue will be recognized separately each month as earned under the accrual basis. No tax applies to financial transactions (loan principal).

output_debit_corrector.reason: Loan disbursement increases Loans Receivable (asset) and decreases Chequing Account (asset). Interest revenue will be recognized separately each month as earned under the accrual basis. No tax applies to financial transactions (loan principal).
